body:
Because Terry needs help defending voting rights
| | |
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/37710487/312340983/-2023814932?refcode=20211019.9_SA_10FC&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzU5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1NTIxM2Y0MS00NTMxLWVjMTEtOTgyMC1jODk2NjUzYjI2YzgiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=5FVJbgc4GUVP8bu7WlcKnr4DFfvcSk-FiPckuWzBBjw=)

| | Derek, I was in Virginia because Terry McAuliffe needs our help.

We know the path to electing more Democrats is mobilizing more people to vote — and there’s good news on that front in Virginia: it has never been easier to vote thanks to the incredible progress made by the Democrats in Virginia’s state government. We can protect this progress by electing Terry.

**So far, though, not enough voters have cast their ballots in this crucial election against Trumpism. And Democrats only have 14 days to turn around that worrying trend with investment in ads, organizers and events across Virginia.**

clean_body: Because Terry needs help defending voting rights | | | = | | Derek, I was in Virginia because Terry McAuliffe needs our help. We know the path to electing more Democrats is mobilizing more people to vote — and there’s good news on that front in Virginia: it has never been easier to vote thanks to the incredible progress made by the Democrats in Virginia’s state government. We can protect this progress by electing Terry. So far, though, not enough voters have cast their ballots in this crucial election against Trumpism. And Democrats only have 14 days to turn around that worrying trend with investment in ads, organizers and events across Virginia. = | | | = ** | = | | = | = | | = | = We have seen the danger of Donald Trump’s rhetoric and the threat it poses to our democracy. That’s why it’s horrifying to hear Terry’s opponent, Glenn Youngkin, repeating the same talking points and lies as a strategy to motivate conspiracy theorists to go to the polls. Derek, we need leaders who stand up for our democracy and voting access — not extremists who will restrict voting access and defend Donald Trump’s conspiracy theories. Right now, the Virginia governor’s race is Trumpism’s best chance to mount a comeback, and I’m asking you to join me in standing with Terry McAuliffe and against Donald Trump. = Thank you for being a champion of voting rights, Stacey Abrams =** Terry McAuliffe for Virginia PO Box 26112 Arlington, VA 22215 If you would like to opt-out of future emails, click. Paid for by Terry for Virginia

name: Tim Ryan
subject: Meeting voters where they are
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tim Ryan for Ohio
body:
[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/46?t=1001&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ][IMG]

Hi folks,

These days I spend a lot of time on the campaign trail. The hours are long
but there’s nothing more rewarding. I get to drive across beautiful Ohio
and meet people face to face. And I love talking to voters and hearing
what issues matter most to them.

[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5397?t=1007&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Collage of Tim meeting voters

I’m not just going to Democratic strongholds either -- I’m going to the
deep red pockets of our state because I know every voice deserves to be
heard. But I need resources to stay on the trail in this dead heat race:

[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5397?t=1008&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Can you chip in $25 to help me continue to meet voters where they are?

[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5397?t=1009&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Donate $25 [ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5398?t=1010&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Donate $50
[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5399?t=1011&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Donate $100 [ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5400?t=1012&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Donate $200
[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5401?t=1013&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Donate $250 [ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5397?t=1014&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Other Amount

Our campaign is covering the whole state -- from Ashtabula to Lima, Akron
to Portsmouth and nearly everywhere in between -- meeting voters where
they are. Whether I’m in the local diner, on a factory floor, sitting in a
union hall, or hitting the county fair, I make it my priority to hear from
everyone.

Folks, you can bet my opponents aren’t going around the state talking to
working Ohioans. They spend their time rubbing elbows with their big-money
donors and attending Trumped-up fundraisers.

It’s clear they don’t have Ohio families’ best interests in mind. That’s
why it’s so important for me to go to every corner, every forgotten small
town in our state to see how I can best serve Ohio as Senator.

But I need your help. If I want to spend more time talking to voters and
less time raising money, we need to meet our fundraising benchmarks.
Unlike my opponents, I spend my time talking to real folks -- not
out-of-touch, out-of-state billionaires.

This race is shaping up to be the most expensive race ever in Ohio -- but
I didn’t get in this fight to cozy up to big-money backers -- that’s why I
rely on small dollar donations from folks like you.

[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5397?t=1015&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]So, can I count on you to chip in and keep me on the trail focusing on
voters, not donors?

[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5397?t=1016&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Donate $25 [ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5398?t=1017&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Donate $50
[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5399?t=1018&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Donate $100 [ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5400?t=1019&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Donate $200
[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5401?t=1020&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Donate $250 [ https://act.timforoh.com/go/5401?t=1021&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Other Amount

I appreciate your support,

Tim

[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/47?t=1002&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ][IMG]
We believe that emails are the best way for our organization to stay in
touch with supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails,
[ https://act.timforoh.com/signup/Fewer_Emails/?t=1003&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]click here.
If you'd like to make a donation to our campaign, you can do that below!
[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/1774?t=1004&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Donate Monthly
[ https://act.timforoh.com/go/49?t=1005&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ]Donate Once

Paid for by Tim Ryan for Ohio
Tim Ryan for Ohio
P.O. Box 189
Niles, Ohio 44446
United States
[ mailto:info@timforoh.com ][IMG] [ https://act.timforoh.com/go/11?t=1006&akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA ][IMG]

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.timforoh.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1198%2E3611125%2ELX_cWA

body:
2 weeks until polls close, . | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/39f439658a78/how-to-volunteer-this-weekend-5025829?e=400a181d5a)
---
[](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=d5bd021367&e=400a181d5a)
Hi ,We’re only two weeks out from Election Day, and we need your help reminding your neighbors to go vote! We have hundreds of Get Out The Vote (aka **GOTV**) volunteer shifts to fill between now and Election Day, so I need you to sign up and bring your friends!Wherever there is a Democratic sample ballot, we are carrying those at every canvass! So by coming out to volunteer, you’ll be helping your local school board, city council, and township trustee candidates as well as Allison Russo. **It’s all hands on deck for the next two weeks, . Are you in?**
## _Knock on Doors with Local Democrats:_
_Click to sign-up and share with friends:_
* [**Athens County GOTV Canvass**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=2e26eb8641&e=400a181d5a)
* [**Canal Winchester GOTV Canvass**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=1e401f33f1&e=400a181d5a)
* _**WITH FRANKLIN CO. RECORDER DANNY O’CONNOR THIS SATURDAY AT NOON!**_
* [**Columbus City Coordinated Campaign GOTV Canvass**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=9f7dc02e7e&e=400a181d5a)
* [**Grove City/SWCSD Coordinated GOTV Canvass**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=42c88b8185&e=400a181d5a)
* [**Hilliard Coordinated Campaign GOTV Canvass**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=bd39855140&e=400a181d5a)
* [**Lancaster GOTV Canvass**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=9a4cea814b&e=400a181d5a)
* [**Madison County GOTV Canvass**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=5f8726ba63&e=400a181d5a)
* [**Morgan County GOTV Canvass**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=fce74f77dd&e=400a181d5a)
* [**Pickerington/Violet Twp. Coordinated Campaign GOTV**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=342dc5f624&e=400a181d5a)
* [**Ross County GOTV Canvass**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=51939d13b5&e=400a181d5a)
* [**Upper Arlington/NW Columbus Coordinated Campaign GOTV**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=274dc3a618&e=400a181d5a)
* _**WITH CONGRESSMAN TIM RYAN THIS SATURDAY AT 10!**_
* [**Victorian Village GOTV Canvass**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=a58729e966&e=400a181d5a)
* _**WITH STATE REP. KRISTIN BOGGS THIS SATURDAY AT 10!**_**
**

| [VIEW ALL CANVASSES](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=2acc748cc6&e=400a181d5a)
---
## _Make Phone Calls and Send Texts:_
* [**GOTV Virtual Phone Bank**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=602fcb0f9f&e=400a181d5a)**with OPAL!**
* _Trainings are Sunday at 4pm and Tuesday at 6pm_
* _Once trained, make calls every day of the week!_
* [**Rural Voters for Russo Virtual Phone Bank**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=fa4f7885cb&e=400a181d5a)
* _Trainings are Monday and Wednesday at 6pm_
* _Once trained, make calls every day of the week!_
* [**Seniors for Russo Virtual Phone Bank**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=4e70e55074&e=400a181d5a)
* _Trainings are Tuesday and Thursday at 5pm_
* _Once trained, make calls every day of the week!_
* [**Students for Russo Virtual Phone Bank**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=30eea4a820&e=400a181d5a)
* _Trainings are Tuesday and Thursday at 5pm_
* _Once trained, make calls every day of the week!_
* [**GOTV Text Banks!**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=8faabcc56f&e=400a181d5a)
* We have shifts this week, next week, and Election Day!

| [VIEW ALL PHONE BANKS](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=ac22e294f6&e=400a181d5a)
---
## _Write Letters to Neighbors:_
Research shows that neighbor-to-neighbor conversations are some of the most impactful political conversations you can have. The campaign can pay for flashy mailers and TV commercials, but [**only YOU can help us send a letter to your folks.**](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=31419c72f0&e=400a181d5a) We'll ask you to write a short note on each letter, and to hand-address each envelope to our targeted voter list. This, along with a return address folks can recognize, makes a huge difference.
_Letters should be mailed no later than October 27th_
| [VIEW TEMPLATE LETTER AND SIGN UP](https://Allisonrusso.us6.list-manage.com/track/click?u=8a3818e62f1e6e7a398f3ef29&id=2b4502701e&e=400a181d5a)

clean_body: 2 weeks until polls close, . | | | Hi ,We’re only two weeks out from Election Day, and we need your help reminding your neighbors to go vote! We have hundreds of Get Out The Vote (aka GOTV) volunteer shifts to fill between now and Election Day, so I need you to sign up and bring your friends!Wherever there is a Democratic sample ballot, we are carrying those at every canvass! So by coming out to volunteer, you’ll be helping your local school board, city council, and township trustee candidates as well as Allison Russo. It’s all hands on deck for the next two weeks, . Are you in? Knock on Doors with Local Democrats: Click to sign-up and share with friends: WITH FRANKLIN CO. RECORDER DANNY O’CONNOR THIS SATURDAY AT NOON! WITH CONGRESSMAN TIM RYAN THIS SATURDAY AT 10! WITH STATE REP. KRISTIN BOGGS THIS SATURDAY AT 10! | Make Phone Calls and Send Texts: OPAL! Trainings are Sunday at 4pm and Tuesday at 6pm Once trained, make calls every day of the week! Trainings are Monday and Wednesday at 6pm Once trained, make calls every day of the week! Trainings are Tuesday and Thursday at 5pm Once trained, make calls every day of the week! Trainings are Tuesday and Thursday at 5pm Once trained, make calls every day of the week! * We have shifts this week, next week, and Election Day! | Write Letters to Neighbors: Research shows that neighbor-to-neighbor conversations are some of the most impactful political conversations you can have. The campaign can pay for flashy mailers and TV commercials, but We'll ask you to write a short note on each letter, and to hand-address each envelope to our targeted voter list. This, along with a return address folks can recognize, makes a huge difference. Letters should be mailed no later than October 27th | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | | | Copyright (C) 2021 RUSSO FOR CONGRESS. All rights reserved.

name: Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez
subject: My tips on burnout
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress
body:
I’ve experienced burnout in both big and small episodes, and having been there and back a few times, here’s what I’ve learned:
# [](https://act.ocasiocortez.com/go/143?refcode=em20211019-2595-header&t=1&akid=2595%2E2526796%2E6VotxS)
Earlier this week, I posted an AMA — Ask Me Anything — on Instagram, and received a question about burnout.
Burnout, especially in these times, seems to have become extremely common. When there is so much happening in the world around you and in your direct sphere of influence, it can be especially overwhelming. **That’s why I wanted to share some of the tips I’ve learned here about recovering from and preventing burnout.**
First off, if you’re feeling burnout — I’m sorry. Burnout is awful, and especially hard to manage because it’s hard to figure out that 1) you’re burnt out and 2) what to do about it. I’ve experienced burnout in both big and small episodes, and having been there and back a few times, here’s what I’ve learned:
1. It’s important to create healthy expectations and compassion for yourself when recovering from burnout. This sucks, but burnout can take a long time to recover from. In some cases it can take weeks, months, or even years — but don’t panic. No matter how burnt out you are, you can recover.
2. Burnout has a lot of contributing factors — it’s not just working long hours (though that can be a contributor). It’s much deeper than that. Think of your whole self as a cup. Participating in certain activities that are physically, mentally, spiritually, and emotionally demanding means that you’re pouring from your cup. These may be activities you choose and even love, but you’re still pouring from your cup to participate in them.
A healthy balance is when you both _fill_ and pour from your cup. When you do things you’ve always wanted to do, or that bring you joy and are just for you — you fill your cup. But when you’re obligated to fulfill mentally, physically, spiritually, or emotionally demanding work that crowds out any time or energy for you to do things that fill your cup, your cup runs dry — and that’s burnout.
It can happen over months and years, or during shorter but highly traumatic periods. So what do you do?

Recovering and healing from burnout is really hard, because while you may be able to get some rest — rest alone won’t heal it. You need to start doing the opposite of what got you here: **which means you’ve got a prescription for indulgence and strong boundaries.**
1. For the indulgence piece, recovering from burnout is about replenishing your energy and carving out time for YOURSELF. You need to refill your energy bar — both physically and spiritually/mentally. If you’re physically exhausted, you need to spend time being a total potato in bed for hours and not feel guilty about it. But if you’re spiritually/mentally drained, then you need to write a list of things you selfishly want to do just for you.
They could be small things like cooking a nice meal, getting your nails done, or playing hooky with a friend for a day, or big things like scratching off a bucket list item. And start prioritizing them. Put them on your schedule. Cancel other things so you can do these, because if you don’t start refilling your cup, things will only get worse. Filling your cup is your job now.
2. On boundaries, there’s a lot of stuff we do because we feel like we have to, even if we don't actually have to do it. Some of this “have to” comes from ourselves and the pressure we put on ourselves, but some of it also comes from other people who may use guilt to get things from you. Feeling guilty is intense, and it can feel much easier in the moment to pour from your cup in order to avoid guilt, so we just say “yes” to everything. Not anymore. Your standard for saying yes just got much higher. You need to delegate, cancel, and ask for help. Get those things off your calendar. You might upset some people, but that’s something you need to get used to.
I say this for myself as well, because I get guilted into doing things too. It’s my theory that women and people of all genders who are raised and programmed to give, get burnt out more because we’re not taught to say “no.” People will always feel entitled to you and your time to either avoid pouring from their own cup or to fill theirs up. Sometimes they have no idea it’s your last drop. That’s why you need to learn to say “no.” If you continue to say “yes” and you betray yourself to pour more from your cup, eventually your body will say “no” for you — you could get sick or have an accident. Your health is more important.
When you’re burnt out, you need to consciously be working to fill your cup more than you pour out. It can be hard, but you need to try to get to a 1-to-1 ratio. Here’s my advice: don’t think of work or other commitments as one big pour. We make lots of decisions at work or for our families. Start with microscopic decisions that reduce the pour. Does that meeting really need to be 30 minutes, or could 15 minutes or maybe an email suffice? If you don’t have a desk job and instead work shifts, can you start committing to an after-shift activity for yourself that’s not happy hour? Can a family member be doing more? Do your kids need you to do something for them, or have they gotten used to you doing something for them?
Just start to ask these questions and assess. You need to be like the IRS in spiritually auditing the use of your time and energy. It may feel ruthless and selfish at first, but consider the alternative of potentially developing a chronic illness or a panic attack.

Also, no matter what you do, you always need to have something to look forward to. Having nothing to look forward to creates real despair. So schedule that haircut, book a yoga class, or put a “Do not disturb 8-9PM” on your door when you get home and just watercolor, journal, or whatever you want. You need scheduled things to look forward to. I found that when I’ve pre-planned time off, blocked it off on my calendar and scheduled around it, my life started to feel way more manageable. When I started to let that practice slip, it felt overwhelming again.
And make sure you communicate where you’re at with the people asking for things from you, so that they can understand and start to help you out.
Like I said, recovering from burnout may not be easy, but it is possible. Carve out that time for yourself and fill your cup. You can do this.
Take care,
Alexandria

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end. Small donors like you keep us going. To contribute via check, please address to Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez for Congress, PO Box 680080, Corona, NY 11368.

name: Charles Booker
subject: Rand Paul shared how he really feels about poor people.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Booker for Kentucky
body:
[1]Charles Booker

Peter — Rand Paul
spoke in front of Congress and shared how he really feels about poor
people.

“The poorest people in our country actually are equivalent to the middle
class in most countries. We are a huge success!” – Rand Paul

Shameful. I grew up poor. I come from the struggle. I will not accept or
tolerate an ignorant politician telling the people of Kentucky that we
should be satisfied with poverty.

Rand Paul has never had his lights cut off or been homeless. Rand Paul has
never rationed his medicine to survive. Rand Paul doesn’t know a damn
thing about our struggles because he doesn’t see us. He thinks he’s better
than us.

That’s why we’re rising up together, fighting for the kind of leader
Kentucky deserves, someone who knows what it's like to worry about money
every single day. [ https://act.charlesbooker.org/go/11647?refcode=em20211019-1074&amp%3Bamounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1020&akid=1074%2E346206%2EThkhjk ]If you’re with me in this fight, can you make
a $5 donation right now to our campaign?

[ https://act.charlesbooker.org/go/11647?refcode=em20211019-1074&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1021&akid=1074%2E346206%2EThkhjk ]Contribute $5

Unfortunately,
Peter, Rand Paul has been
putting our lives at risk from the moment he took office.

He’s rejected health care funds for 9/11 first responders. He voted
against vital funds — billions of dollars — that would go to our
Commonwealth for things like roads, bridges, and broadband. He continues
to double down on spreading misinformation about COVID-19 to benefit from
keeping Kentuckians sick.

In our darkest crises, Rand still finds a way to mock our struggle.

Enough is enough. We’re building a campaign that represents ALL of us, not
just the wealthy few. But building this kind of movement takes a lot of
resources and funding.

[ https://act.charlesbooker.org/go/11647?refcode=em20211019-1074&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1022&akid=1074%2E346206%2EThkhjk ]If you’re ready to end this chapter of failed leadership, will you
make a donation right now and show your support for our historic movement
to turn Kentucky blue? Together, we have the power to shock the world.

Let’s win this,

Charles

Contribute to Charles Booker: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cb-email-donate?refcode=em20211019-1074-footer&t=1023&refcode2=1074_346206_Thkhjk&akid=1074%2E346206%2EThkhjk

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe:

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.charlesbooker.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=1074%2E346206%2EThkhjk

Contributions to Booker for Kentucky are not deductible for federal income tax purposes.

Paid for by Booker for Kentucky

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: ICYMI: Summer Lee is running for Congress
body:
Launch day is almost over, Peter
#
Peter,
We can’t let today go by without providing you with an update on Summer Lee’s campaign launch. The grassroots movement backing Summer has shown up today, but we’re still short of the $50,000 we need to raise within the first 24 hours of Summer announcing her campaign.
These first few days are critical, Peter. As a progressive primary challenger, Summer needs to prove to the Democratic establishment that she has the resources to run a competitive campaign. Most Congressional candidates are expected to raise thousands of dollars in the first weeks just from friends and family, but Summer isn’t backed by the ultrawealthy, which is why she is counting on grassroots donors to step up and give what they can.
**[We are so excited for all of the support for Summer Lee’s campaign for Congress, but we are still $11,539 short of our goal to raise $50,000 by the end of today. Will you chip in and become a founding donor to Summer’s campaign?](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/78030?refcode=em20211019-20098&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=20098%2E1203867%2EOFkgfB)**
**[Become a Founding Donor](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/78030?refcode=em20211019-20098&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=20098%2E1203867%2EOFkgfB)**
Summer Lee is running for Congress to really represent the people of Southwestern Pennsylvania and to fight for Medicare for All, a Green New Deal, an end to student debt, overdue solutions to fix the unjust criminal legal system, and much more.
The past few weeks, we’ve seen the power of progressive house members, unapologetically standing for the people’s interests. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Pramila Jayapal, Rashida Talib, Cori Bush, and Jamaal Bowman are doing what they can.**But in order to fight for the people’s interests and actually get progressive priorities passed, we need to elect more members of Congress like them.**
We need to hit our $50,000 goal in order to invest in the field, communications, and finance strategies needed to win. [**That’s why we need to ask that before the day ends, will you rush a donation directly to Summer Lee? When you chip in these first 24 hours, you’re building the campaign operation needed to get Summer elected.](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/78030?refcode=em20211019-20098&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&akid=20098%2E1203867%2EOFkgfB)**
**[Contribute](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/78030?refcode=em20211019-20098&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=23&akid=20098%2E1203867%2EOFkgfB)**
In solidarity,
Justice Democrats

Do not worry if you cannot afford to make a contribution — we understand that this is a difficult time. If you’re struggling, you can find a food bank [here.](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/28883?t=24&akid=20098%2E1203867%2EOFkgfB) We appreciate everything you do to keep our movement strong. Please stay informed and follow the most up-to-date recommendations from the [Centers for Disease Control](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/24438?t=25&akid=20098%2E1203867%2EOFkgfB) and your state public health department.
---

**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20211019-20098-footer&t=26&refcode2=20098_1203867_OFkgfB&akid=20098%2E1203867%2EOFkgfB)**
---
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Federal candidates or officeholders mentioned above are not asking for more than $5,000 per calendar year from individuals or other federally permissible sources, nor are they asking for any funds from corporations, labor unions, or any other federally prohibited sources.
JusticeDemocrats.com
10629 Hardin Valley Rd, #226, Knoxville, TN 37932
Email us: [us@justicedemocrats.com](mailto:us@justicedemocrats.com)

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=27&akid=20098%2E1203867%2EOFkgfB)
* [Unsubscribe](https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=28&akid=20098%2E1203867%2EOFkgfB)

name: Team WisGOP
subject: WisGOP Update
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE REPUBLICAN PARTY OF WISCONSIN
body:
The latest news from the Republican Party of Wisconsin

| | [](https://act.wisgop.org/r?u=BJAgHetUUIqrbBbKj3z09j9OpIZbBzc5g2-7VwfHf34&e=d00ac07d22940c0f36f888b7fa3c8df2&utm_source=wisgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter_10_19_2021_v4&n=1)
---
### October 19, 2021
Friend -- Happy Fall! We hope you are enjoying the transition to the fall season. Here at the Republican Party of Wisconsin, we are continuing to ramp up our efforts ahead of 2022: training and preparing for local elections, working to secure election integrity, working alongside the RNC to staff up our field program, and making sure that we are holding the Democrats accountable. In addition to the WisGOP Update, we recently launched a new email series, the Weekend Roundup! These approximately biweekly emails highlight the outstanding work that county parties are doing across the state as they host community events and rally the troops ahead of 2022\. Make sure to keep an eye out in your inbox! You can read more about what we’re working on here at the WisGOP below. \- Team WisGOP | Have you ever considered running for public office? As liberals try to impose their ideology on local governments and school boards across the country, the WisGOP is supporting county parties to fight back, including in the Mequon-Thiensville School Board School Board recall election on November 2nd. While Democrats support far-left proposals like critical race theory and mandatory masks in schools, Republicans will always support parent choice. Sign up to get involved to help take back our local governments [**here**](https://act.wisgop.org/r?u=sxV_uZjhTYiS0NkYk0dD0eXv_5DaexUSZI9PlfXgIO8&e=d00ac07d22940c0f36f888b7fa3c8df2&utm_source=wisgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter_10_19_2021_v4&n=2) , or sign up to run for office [**here**](https://act.wisgop.org/r?u=sxV_uZjhTYiS0NkYk0dD0Ys_OtaW8UVXc21-DAX8Kwpg8-KKEZDexXV0vLTBoAwu&e=d00ac07d22940c0f36f888b7fa3c8df2&utm_source=wisgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter_10_19_2021_v4&n=3) to make sure that we have patriots fighting back against the far-left fringe at every level! The WisGOP continues to fight for election integrity to ensure that all Wisconsinites can have confidence in the results of our elections. Make sure that you are staying updated on our Election Integrity Dashboard (located [**here**](https://act.wisgop.org/r?u=sxV_uZjhTYiS0NkYk0dD0eH7mQqnwg7TiRkzkxQsBEjYN0qYIVr_dl05xpqOQV0I&e=d00ac07d22940c0f36f888b7fa3c8df2&utm_source=wisgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter_10_19_2021_v4&n=4)) to read a recap of some of our election integrity efforts and catch up on the latest updates on the election integrity bills put forward by Republicans in the State Legislature. | | In case you missed it, last month the Republican Party of Wisconsin filed a complaint against the Minocqua Brewing Company Super PAC with the Federal Elections Commission for a myriad of reporting failures. This complaint is separate and in addition to another filed by the Marathon County GOP. While the Minocqua Brewing Company purports to send 5 percent of their profits to their PAC, they fail to report any such transfer, or to disclose these donors in campaign finance reports. The complaint alleges the Super PAC has also failed to include required disclaimer notices on solicitations for donations, failed to file required reports for an independent expenditure, and unlawfully used corporate funds to provide direct support to federal candidates. Read more from Wisconsin Right Now [**here**](https://act.wisgop.org/r?u=5wg3don5l6_J12hXkf3HPcNYT6lUz2m18CPWRl8AJrxCoIOsQip5zDYCIqIhfu6qFH1j4EiZtg-TPiGZpT0oChce1qGCuU_MqsGmcsQIlXTyn0ihqcxBGqY2GyxgZs4v&e=d00ac07d22940c0f36f888b7fa3c8df2&utm_source=wisgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter_10_19_2021_v4&n=5). Mayor Tom Barrett’s budget [**proposal**](https://act.wisgop.org/r?u=FY_5R3yFZls9oXq6sZQ0zb6UZ59JIWB3I0rLtfLcCl08RjUZufY-Azp9RA34xlWx5XGA1Z15-fOKbWg2BYe_dSS1HtdWznl2LNB5TPk2gZyYIu_QxjEzylIyxib9X1NLpB86sb4AYNzcT5kiXCVDkQ&e=d00ac07d22940c0f36f888b7fa3c8df2&utm_source=wisgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter_10_19_2021_v4&n=6) includes a cut of 25 law enforcement officers at the Milwaukee Police Department. This comes after the city cut 60 and 120 officers over the past two years. Despite the efforts of liberals like Tony Evers, who half-heartedly [**pretend**](https://act.wisgop.org/r?u=sxV_uZjhTYiS0NkYk0dD0fqtrgpLQa-tOqYgqrdjPaTMJaGv0gq_yTAvzLHEyD4JBZiBzUcLQ4jqoSzj3gDWwBaMBVylrkYyBYUxCu8tOMqMsaBGbeVapLwBzfwSKKQb&e=d00ac07d22940c0f36f888b7fa3c8df2&utm_source=wisgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter_10_19_2021_v4&n=7) to support law enforcement while simultaneously failing to stop cities from defunding the police, the fact remains that Democrats will always choose the far-left fringe over public safety.
---|---|---
* * *
[](https://act.wisgop.org/r?u=sxV_uZjhTYiS0NkYk0dD0ahw3PTL6JsP8slQyv_Z45W1tpC4bxcjwcY7pLDfF8cTNlZrezZB2oVgQgpQzhDJdQ&e=d00ac07d22940c0f36f888b7fa3c8df2&utm_source=wisgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter_10_19_2021_v4&n=8) | | Want to help stand up for conservative principles in your community? Submit a Letter to the Editor! You can send a letter directly to your local paper by filling out the form on our **[website](https://act.wisgop.org/r?u=sxV_uZjhTYiS0NkYk0dD0YlDWo7N8G4qrBHsPwAeY7TAgGRpg7tf5_CGWVX6mHSO&e=d00ac07d22940c0f36f888b7fa3c8df2&utm_source=wisgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter_10_19_2021_v4&n=9)**.
---|---|---
* * *
### **In Case You Missed It...**
### _[**Two Years Into Crime Spike, Tony Evers Pretends to Care About Public Safety**](https://act.wisgop.org/r?u=sxV_uZjhTYiS0NkYk0dD0fqtrgpLQa-tOqYgqrdjPaTMJaGv0gq_yTAvzLHEyD4JBZiBzUcLQ4jqoSzj3gDWwBaMBVylrkYyBYUxCu8tOMqMsaBGbeVapLwBzfwSKKQb&e=d00ac07d22940c0f36f888b7fa3c8df2&utm_source=wisgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter_10_19_2021_v4&n=10)_
Recently Tony Evers dipped into his taxpayer-funded re-election slush fund, pretending to care about public safety by vaguely designating ARPA funds in support of “violence prevention.” After a year of historic crime, with Milwaukee and Madison nearly topping the nation in homicides and Josh Kaul’s botched DOJ crime lab failing in key areas, Evers’ strategy to throw federal money at the problem is too little, too late.
### _[**On National Farmers Day, Democrats’ Ag Policies Plant Seeds of Doubt for Rural Voters**](https://act.wisgop.org/r?u=sxV_uZjhTYiS0NkYk0dD0TxHPdvvwghLxgJYe0vJV21fkKgCVwNJJ4rFEKtxDraJsSJS84OSFmoC2xQLVByYCDrSBWNg4oNETmGuJ9hSSQMY2OsahbZwLE7Tl3Z3SgZYej_UFgd8Ys2iE-G65YGiAQ&e=d00ac07d22940c0f36f888b7fa3c8df2&utm_source=wisgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter_10_19_2021_v4&n=11)_
The Republican Party of Wisconsin joins the nation in celebrating National Farmers Day as we honor the hardworking men and women in agriculture who feed our country. While Republicans stand up for farmers and rural communities, Democrats seek to dismantle rural Wisconsinites’ way of life — leading to a widespread shift of rural Americans away from the Democrat Party.
### _[**Democrats Ring in Crime Prevention Month With Public Safety Failures**](https://act.wisgop.org/r?u=sxV_uZjhTYiS0NkYk0dD0TkWDZThKqqIQl3o7WoOyFLFTfItdE9xd_CwhbEbaVgyLovpfRKbB-rGoFe4qEHi1vhh8Q00JQdQfA_vlGNI1RlNJ4-whIhiERiaRSGEoyU2&e=d00ac07d22940c0f36f888b7fa3c8df2&utm_source=wisgop&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=newsletter_10_19_2021_v4&n=12)_
This October, the Republican Party of Wisconsin is proud to celebrate Crime Prevention Month as we thank the law enforcement officers and first responders who protect our communities. Meanwhile, Democrats instead protect the “woke” mob, violent criminals, and the far-left fringe — making Wisconsin families less safe. As a result, crime continues to rise in cities like Milwaukee and Madison following the “most deadly year in history.”

clean_body: The latest news from the Republican Party of Wisconsin | | October 19, 2021 Friend -- Happy Fall! We hope you are enjoying the transition to the fall season. Here at the Republican Party of Wisconsin, we are continuing to ramp up our efforts ahead of 2022: training and preparing for local elections, working to secure election integrity, working alongside the RNC to staff up our field program, and making sure that we are holding the Democrats accountable. In addition to the WisGOP Update, we recently launched a new email series, the Weekend Roundup! These approximately biweekly emails highlight the outstanding work that county parties are doing across the state as they host community events and rally the troops ahead of 2022\. Make sure to keep an eye out in your inbox! You can read more about what we’re working on here at the WisGOP below. \- Team WisGOP | Have you ever considered running for public office? As liberals try to impose their ideology on local governments and school boards across the country, the WisGOP is supporting county parties to fight back, including in the Mequon-Thiensville School Board School Board recall election on November 2nd. While Democrats support far-left proposals like critical race theory and mandatory masks in schools, Republicans will always support parent choice. Sign up to get involved to help take back our local governments , or sign up to run for office to make sure that we have patriots fighting back against the far-left fringe at every level! The WisGOP continues to fight for election integrity to ensure that all Wisconsinites can have confidence in the results of our elections. Make sure that you are staying updated on our Election Integrity Dashboard (located ) to read a recap of some of our election integrity efforts and catch up on the latest updates on the election integrity bills put forward by Republicans in the State Legislature. | | In case you missed it, last month the Republican Party of Wisconsin filed a complaint against the Minocqua Brewing Company Super PAC with the Federal Elections Commission for a myriad of reporting failures. This complaint is separate and in addition to another filed by the Marathon County GOP. While the Minocqua Brewing Company purports to send 5 percent of their profits to their PAC, they fail to report any such transfer, or to disclose these donors in campaign finance reports. The complaint alleges the Super PAC has also failed to include required disclaimer notices on solicitations for donations, failed to file required reports for an independent expenditure, and unlawfully used corporate funds to provide direct support to federal candidates. Read more from Wisconsin Right Now . Mayor Tom Barrett’s budget includes a cut of 25 law enforcement officers at the Milwaukee Police Department. This comes after the city cut 60 and 120 officers over the past two years. Despite the efforts of liberals like Tony Evers, who half-heartedly to support law enforcement while simultaneously failing to stop cities from defunding the police, the fact remains that Democrats will always choose the far-left fringe over public safety. ---|---|--- | | Want to help stand up for conservative principles in your community? Submit a Letter to the Editor! You can send a letter directly to your local paper by filling out the form on our . ---|---|--- In Case You Missed It... Recently Tony Evers dipped into his taxpayer-funded re-election slush fund, pretending to care about public safety by vaguely designating ARPA funds in support of “violence prevention.” After a year of historic crime, with Milwaukee and Madison nearly topping the nation in homicides and Josh Kaul’s botched DOJ crime lab failing in key areas, Evers’ strategy to throw federal money at the problem is too little, too late. The Republican Party of Wisconsin joins the nation in celebrating National Farmers Day as we honor the hardworking men and women in agriculture who feed our country. While Republicans stand up for farmers and rural communities, Democrats seek to dismantle rural Wisconsinites’ way of life — leading to a widespread shift of rural Americans away from the Democrat Party. This October, the Republican Party of Wisconsin is proud to celebrate Crime Prevention Month as we thank the law enforcement officers and first responders who protect our communities. Meanwhile, Democrats instead protect the “woke” mob, violent criminals, and the far-left fringe — making Wisconsin families less safe. As a result, crime continues to rise in cities like Milwaukee and Madison following the “most deadly year in history.” Worth the Read | | ---|---|--- | | Top Sharable Content

name: Team Jayapal
subject: The fight continues
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Pramila for Congress
body:
It’s already been a big week for Pramila — take a look:
#
Peter, it's only Tuesday and it's already been a BIG week for Pramila and our movement as we fight to deliver for working people across America!
First, this happened:
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20211019?refcode=em20211019-733&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&refcode2=733_420207_JP29wC&akid=733%2E420207%2EJP29wC)
Then, this:
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20211019?refcode=em20211019-733&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&refcode2=733_420207_JP29wC&akid=733%2E420207%2EJP29wC)
And now, Pramila is continuing her urgent fight to get President Biden's entire Build Back Better agenda over the finish line — so we can make a real difference in people's lives. **[If you're ready to deliver bold action on climate, health care, paid leave, housing, childcare, and more to millions of working families — all while making corporations and the ultra-rich finally pay their fair share — can you rush a contribution of $3 or more today to help Pramila hold the line?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20211019?refcode=em20211019-733&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&refcode2=733_420207_JP29wC&akid=733%2E420207%2EJP29wC) **
**[Contribute $3 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20211019?refcode=em20211019-733&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=22&refcode2=733_420207_JP29wC&akid=733%2E420207%2EJP29wC)**
The policies that Pramila is working around the clock to pass in the Build Back Better Act are necessary, urgent, and POPULAR:
[📈](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/8200?t=23&akid=733%2E420207%2EJP29wC) 83% of Americans want Medicare to negotiate lower drug prices
[📈](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/8200?t=24&akid=733%2E420207%2EJP29wC) 73% support paid leave
[📈](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/go/8200?t=25&akid=733%2E420207%2EJP29wC) 67% are in favor of universal pre-k
Voters gave Democrats the White House, the Senate, and the House — and now, they're making it clear: **You need to deliver the ENTIRE Build Back Better agenda.**
[**Support Pramila's fight for the entire Build Back Better agenda by rushing a contribution today. Every dollar puts our movement in a stronger position to win what we’ve worked so hard for: real, transformative change.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-20211019?refcode=em20211019-733&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=26&refcode2=733_420207_JP29wC&akid=733%2E420207%2EJP29wC)
In solidarity,
Team Jayapal

**[Contribute](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pj-email-footer?refcode=em20211019-733-footer&t=27&refcode2=733_420207_JP29wC&akid=733%2E420207%2EJP29wC)**
---
[](https://www.facebook.com/pramila.jayapal) | [](https://twitter.com/pramilajayapal) | [](https://www.instagram.com/pramilajayapal/)
Email us: info@pramilaforcongress.com
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, and small-dollar contributions sustain our movement for transformative change. To contribute by check, please address to Pramila for Congress, PO Box 21912, Seattle, WA 98111.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/signup/did-your-info-change/?t=28&akid=733%2E420207%2EJP29wC)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.pramilaforcongress.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=29&akid=733%2E420207%2EJP29wC)
